modifications.gate: HHF: ENA is always available — Rest GTs do not suppress ENA (HHF 3.4).
HHF: US 9th Army activates on AM GT of Oct 22 (GD 41). Until then, 1st Army controls all US units (HHF 9.2.1, 9.2.2).

patch.appendContent:
### HHF Command Restrictions

**1st US Army (HHF 9.2.1):** Until the AM GT of Oct 22 (GD 41), the US 1st Army controls all US units on the map, including units designated as subordinated to the US 9th Army. On the AM GT of Oct 22, a boundary between 1st and 9th Armies must be established.

**9th US Army (HHF 9.2.2):** Activated on the AM GT of Oct 22 (GD 41). 9th Army HQ and 11th Cavalry (CavGrp) are placed in Maastricht during the Command Phase of that GT. When the US XIII Corps arrives on Nov 8 (GD 58), it must be subordinated to 9th Army. The 102nd Division arriving the same GD must be assigned to XIII Corps.

**V Corps (HHF 9.2.3):** Only the 102nd CavGrp (4 units) and 62/V AFA Btn are allowed on the map when V Corps arrives Oct 5 (GD 24). On the first GT in Oct or Nov that an Allied-initiated lull ends, the Allied player may shift the V Corps boundary northward.

**German Boundaries (HHF 9.2.5):** Corps and army boundaries must be identified the first AM GT after an HQ arrives.
appendNotes: Plan your Oct 22 boundary carefully — once 9th Army activates, any units in the wrong army's ZOP become OoC immediately (HHF 9.2.1, 9.2.2).
V Corps has strict initial unit limits — don't bring extra assets on-map before Oct 31 or the specific release dates in HHF 9.2.3a.

patch.appendContent:
### HHF Logistics Modifications

**US Fuel Points (HHF 16.2.2):** Beginning Nov 15 (GD 65), both US armies are no longer required to use the fuel point logistic procedures.

**British Logistics (HHF 16.2.3):** During all GTs in Sep and Oct, all formations/units assigned to XXX Corps are considered 'Low' Fuel. Beginning Nov 1 (GD 51), all formations/units are considered 'Normal' Fuel.

**Fuhrer Ammo Reserve (HHF 16.2.4):** Beginning Nov 1 (GD 51), the Germans may draw on the Fuhrer Ammo Reserve.

**Lull TP Requirements (HHF 24.2):** During a lull, both sides must maintain minimum TPs on fuel and ammo delivery. Each side must assign at least 1 TP to transport fuel. Allied side can allocate no more than 2 TPs per GD to bring in units from the Reinforcement Pool.
appendNotes: XXX Corps operates at 'Low' Fuel through all of Sep and Oct — plan British offensive operations accordingly, as Low Fuel limits Mech movement (HHF 16.2.3).
After Nov 15, US armies drop fuel point tracking entirely — a significant logistics simplification that frees up planning bandwidth (HHF 16.2.2).

patch.appendContent:
### HHF Construction

**ET-3 Construction (HHF 17.3.3c):** Beginning Sep 26 (GD 15), the German player may replace one ET-2 with one under-construction ET-3 at the beginning of a friendly Construction Phase. This may only be done every other GD. The ET-3 is completed at the beginning of the next friendly Construction Phase.
appendNotes: German ET-3 construction takes two Construction Phases (start + complete) and can only begin every other GD — plan your fortification upgrades early since the Roer line benefits enormously from ET-3s (HHF 17.3.3c).

patch.appendContent:
### HHF Construction

**ET-3 Construction (HHF 17.3.3c):** Beginning Sep 26 (GD 15), the German player may replace one ET-2 with one under-construction ET-3 at the beginning of a friendly Construction Phase. This may only be done every other GD. The ET-3 is completed at the beginning of the next friendly Construction Phase.
appendNotes: This is the Axis Construction Phase where German ET-3 upgrades actually happen — start converting your most critical Roer/Hurtgen ET-2 positions on GD 15 (HHF 17.3.3c).

modifications.gate: HHF Scenario 1: Air Power is NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Weather determination is NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Command rules are NOT used in Bloody Bucket — no command boundaries, no OoC penalties (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Supply, Truck Points, and the full Logistics system are NOT used in Bloody Bucket. All units are automatically in supply (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Fuel Value Determination is NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Unit modes are NOT used except Tactical and Prepared Assault. No Strategic, Refit, or Reserve modes (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Unit modes are NOT used except Tactical and Prepared Assault. No Strategic, Refit, or Reserve modes (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Exploitation Phases are NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Exploitation Phases are NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Exploitation Phases are NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Exploitation Phases are NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Supply Determination is NOT used — all units are automatically in supply (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Supply Determination is NOT used — all units are automatically in supply (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Air Resupply is NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Air Resupply is NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Replacements and Leaders are NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).
HHF Scenario 1: Replacements and Leaders are NOT used in Bloody Bucket (HHF 30.1.0).

appendNotes: Scenario 2: GOSS 9.2.2 (formation integrity) is NOT used — units may operate outside their parent formation without penalty (HHF 31.4.0).

modifications.gate: HHF Scenario 2: The Allied player does NOT use the Logistics Table (GOSS 16.4.4g, 16.4.4h excluded). Simplified logistics apply (HHF 31.4.0).

appendNotes: Scenario 2 simplifies Allied logistics significantly — no Logistics Table means you skip the ADV/fuel/ammo calculations in GOSS 16.4.4g-h (HHF 31.4.0).
US fatigue checks apply GDs 1-3 (Sep 12-14) for units starting on-map — factor this into opening offensive timing (HHF 31.7.0).

modifications.gate: HHF Scenario 2: US units on-map at start must check for fatigue on GDs 1-3 (Sep 12-14) (HHF 31.7.0).

appendNotes: Check US starting units for fatigue during GDs 1-3 — this can disrupt opening attacks if key units become fatigued (HHF 31.7.0).

modifications.gate: HHF Scenario 3: The Allied player does NOT use the Logistics Table (GOSS 16.4.4g excluded). GOSS 17.5.2 (fortress supply) is also excluded (HHF 32.4.0).

appendNotes: Scenario 3 excludes the Allied Logistics Table (16.4.4g) — simpler supply tracking, but German logistics still apply fully (HHF 32.4.0).
VII Corps US attacks are restricted early — check HHF 32.5.0 for specific limitations on which units can attack and when.

modifications.gate: HHF Scenario 4: All GOSS logistics rules (15.0, 16.0) apply in full. This is the most complete logistics scenario (HHF 33.4.0).

appendNotes: This is the first HHF scenario using full GOSS logistics — be prepared for the complete ADV/fuel/ammo pipeline including the Logistics Table (HHF 33.4.0).

modifications.gate: HHF Scenario 4: A carpet bombing mission is required on GD 66 (Nov 16 AM) — see HHF 33.6.0 for the specific carpet bombing rules.

appendNotes: The mandatory carpet bombing on Nov 16 AM is a major event — plan your ground assault axis to exploit the disrupted German positions (HHF 33.6.0).

modifications.gate: HHF Campaign: All GOSS rules apply. Campaign starts at any monthly scenario and plays through Dec 13 (HHF 34.0).

appendNotes: The campaign game uses all GOSS rules — logistics complexity depends on which monthly scenario you start from (HHF 34.0).
